description: New primary or secondary school building. Covers both expansion of existing schools and entirely new establishments.

typical_cost.land_acquisition: site-specific (use DLUHC land value estimates for the region)

typical_benefits.description: GCSE attainment uplift, reduced persistent absence, qualification progression
typical_benefits.monetisation: Use education unit costs from unit-costs-outcomes.json

typical_benefits.description: Reduced journey to school if replacing distant provision
Sports hall, meeting rooms, library used outside school hours
Temporary construction jobs during build phase
Improved physical activity, mental health from modern facilities
typical_benefits.monetisation: QALY or wellbeing valuation (difficult to monetise robustly)

reference_bcr_range.note: Wide range. Higher BCRs from school replacement in areas of acute need. Lower BCRs where existing provision is adequate and the driver is condition/suitability.

typical_risks: Planning permission delays (especially on greenfield or Green Belt sites)
Ground conditions (contamination on brownfield, flood risk)
Construction cost inflation above GDP deflator
Site acquisition costs exceeding benchmark land values
typical_appraisal_period_justification: 60 years reflects the expected economic life of a school building per Green Book supplementary guidance on asset lives. BCIS data shows median school building life of 55-65 years.

publications: BCIS Quarterly Review of Building Prices
DfE Output Specification and Area Guidelines
Green Book Supplementary Guidance: Optimism Bias
